source_jobs.title: CRM Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SO SHALL WE:

We partner with mid‑market owners and executives in the US to drive bottom‑line growth with mutual upside. A rare combination of consulting, full-stack execution and aligned incentives to eliminate what is wrong with the agency model.

We are what remains. We make growth feel easy.

Because your growth is our business.



THE MISSION

We're looking for someone who lives at the intersection of strategy and execution. You think like a growth marketer — obsessing over where leads are dropping off and why — but you build like an engineer, wiring up the CRM, automations, and integrations that make the fix actually happen.

This is not a pure ops role. We need someone who can diagnose a broken funnel, design a multi-touch lifecycle strategy, then roll up their sleeves and implement every workflow, sequence, and webhook required to bring it to life.




WE’RE LOOKING FOR SOMEONE WHO CAN

 - Audit funnels across multiple clients and identify the highest-leverage conversion gaps at every stage

 - Design lifecycle marketing strategies — structured around email, SMS, and iMessage — that move leads from first touch to closed deal with minimal friction

 - Build and maintain the CRM automations that execute those strategies: sequences, lead scoring models, pipeline triggers, and internal alerts that keep salespeople in the right conversation at the right moment

 - Integrate platforms and data sources using webhooks, Zapier, and n8n so nothing falls through the cracks between systems

 - Define and document CRM best practices, naming conventions, and playbooks that the whole team can follow

 - Analyze campaign performance — open rates, conversion rates, deal velocity — then iterate and improve relentlessly




WHAT YOU CAN DO

Strategy

 - Map full customer lifecycle across acquisition, nurture, and retention

 - Identify funnel drop-off points and design conversion plays

 - Define segmentation logic, messaging cadences, and channel mix

 - Run structured A/B tests on sequences and report on results

 - Translate business goals into automation specs a team can build from

Execution

 - Build workflows in GoHighLevel (GHL), HubSpot, Zoho, or ActiveCampaign

 - Set up email, SMS, and iMessage sequences with smart branching logic

 - Wire integrations via REST APIs, webhooks, Zapier, and n8n

 - Read and write JSON; trace webhook payloads and debug broken flows

 - Audit CRM logs and surface root causes fast

TOOLS & STACK

 - CRM: GoHighLevel (preferred), HubSpot, Zoho, ActiveCampaign, or Salesforce — solid fundamentals matter more than the specific platform

 - Automation middleware: n8n, Zapier, native webhooks

 - Channels: Email, SMS, iMessage (Apple Messages for Business or equivalent

 - integrations)

 - Ads & attribution: Meta, Google Ads — understanding how leads enter the funnel and how to close the loop matters

 - Scripting & data: JSON, basic SQL, and comfort digging through API docs (not mandatory)




WHO YOU ARE

 - You've worked hands-on with a CRM for at least 2 years and have real results to show for it

 - You think in systems — if a salesperson is missing follow-ups, you're already sketching the automation that fixes it

 - You're comfortable talking to a CEO about strategy and a developer about an API payload in the same afternoon

 - You own your work end-to-end: scoping, building, testing, documenting, training

 - You have a background in Systems Engineering, Computer Science, Marketing Technology, or equivalent hands-on experience

 - You're methodical — you document what you build, define naming conventions, and leave things better than you found them




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE (FIRST 3–6 MONTHS)

 1. FUNNEL AUDIT: Full lifecycle audit of 2–3 client funnels with documented gaps and a prioritized improvement roadmap
    

 2. AUTOMATION DEPLOYMENT: At least 5 high-impact workflows live — lead scoring, re-engagement, post-call follow-up, no-show recovery — driving ≥10% conversion lift
    

 3

source_jobs.title: Multimedia Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SO SHALL WE:

We partner with mid‑market owners and executives in the US to drive bottom‑line growth with mutual upside. A rare combination of consulting, full-stack execution and aligned incentives to eliminate what is wrong with the agency model.

We are what remains. We make growth feel easy.

Because your growth is our business.




YOUR MISSION

As a Multimedia Designer, you’ll own the creation and execution of visually compelling designs to drive brand consistency and elevate client marketing outcomes. You’ll be trusted with creative autonomy while collaborating closely with account and creative teams to deliver high-impact visual assets. This role blends hands-on design work with thoughtful brand interpretation and requires a sharp eye for detail, aesthetic sensitivity, and strong conceptual thinking to build scalable, innovative visual solutions across diverse clients and channels.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Design and deliver high-impact Paid Media assets (graphics, videos, animations, GIFs) that elevate campaigns and reflect brand identity.

 - Translate performance goals into visually engaging, conversion-focused creative concepts.

 - Maintain high standards of quality, consistency, and brand alignment across every deliverable.

 - Refine and evolve designs through thoughtful iteration, storytelling, and attention to detail.

 - Use performance insights and creative testing to continuously optimize visual outcomes.

 - Collaborate closely with Performance, Copy, and Creative teams to ensure cohesive campaign execution.

 - Communicate proactively on objectives, timelines, and feedback to keep projects on track.

 - Explore new design trends, tools, and formats to keep creative output fresh and effective.

 - Manage multiple projects efficiently, prioritizing work that drives the greatest impact.




WHO YOU ARE:

 - Quality-First Designer: Delivers high-impact visual assets that meet performance goals and brand standards.

 - Detail-Obsessed Finisher: Maintains precision and consistency across all design formats and platforms.

 - High-Velocity Executor: Manages multiple projects efficiently and thrives under tight deadlines.

 - Experiment-Minded Learner: Stays curious, exploring new tools, trends, and creative techniques.

 - Pragmatic Problem Solver: Balances creativity with practicality to produce effective, scalable designs.

 - Collaborative Self-Starter: Works seamlessly with cross-functional teams to bring campaigns to life.

 - Clear Communicator: Shares ideas, timelines, and feedback transparently to keep projects aligned.

 - Dependable Owner: Takes full accountability for quality, delivery, and continuous improvement.




WHY SO SHALL WE?

 - Great salary.

 - Work with great humans in a supportive, growth-oriented team that delivers real results.

 - Your personal development will be done directly with the CEO

 - Amazing office space in Palermo Hollywood

 - Opportunities for career development and professional growth.

 - Unlimited PTO.

source_jobs.title: Performance Marketer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SO SHALL WE:

We partner with mid‑market owners and executives in the US to drive bottom‑line growth with mutual upside. A rare combination of consulting, full-stack execution and aligned incentives to eliminate what is wrong with the agency model.

We are what remains. We make growth feel easy.

Because your growth is our business.




YOUR MISSION

As a Performance Marketer, you’ll lead the strategy, execution, and continuous optimization of paid media campaigns across multiple platforms for key clients. You will be assigned clients and be given complete freedom and support with how to grow those accounts. You’ll be accountable for delivering measurable business outcomes — from acquisition to retention — by combining high-impact creative, strategic media planning, and sharp performance analysis. This role is both strategic and hands-on, and you'll work closely with cross-functional teams and clients to drive scalable, sustainable growth.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Performance Strategy & Execution

 - Develop, launch, and scale data-driven paid media campaigns across Meta, Google, TikTok, LinkedIn, and YouTube, aligned with client KPIs.
   

 - Build full-funnel strategies that span acquisition, retention, and re-engagement, leveraging platform-specific best practices and automations.
   

 - Own media planning, audience segmentation, and budget allocation to maximize performance and ROI.
   

Creative Direction & Optimization

 - Collaborate with creative and CRO teams to develop and iterate on high-converting ad assets (video, static, carousel, UGC) and landing pages.
   

 - Provide creative direction and feedback based on performance data and platform trends.
   

 - Implement structured A/B tests across creative, copy, targeting, and placements to continuously improve results.
   

Data Analysis & Reporting

 - Analyze campaign performance and translate insights into clear, actionable strategies.
   

 - Monitor key metrics (ROAS, CPA, CTR, LTV, etc.) to identify trends, spot inefficiencies, and scale what works.
   

 - Build and maintain dashboards and pacing reports to ensure transparency and alignment with client goals.
   

Client Relationship Management

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for performance strategy and campaign updates. You will work with pod Director and Project Manager to ensure the client is well serviced, however you will be the main voice around performance marketing.
   

 - Deliver regular reports and insights to educate clients and build trust through transparency and results.
   

 - Align media performance with broader business objectives, adapting strategy as needed.
   

Collaboration & Experimentation

 - Partner with CRO, funnels, content, and developers to ensure cohesive, conversion-optimized user journeys.
   

 - Champion a culture of experimentation and test-and-learn frameworks to drive continuous improvement.
   


WHO YOU ARE:

Performance-Oriented & Strategic

 - You understand how to balance tactical execution with strategic thinking to drive meaningful results.

Data-Driven & Curious:

 - You live in dashboards, love testing, and thrive on uncovering insights that lead to action.

Client-Centered Communicator:

 - You’re comfortable translating complex performance data into clear takeaways and next steps for clients.

Collaborative & Proactive:

 - You work well across teams, and you’re always a few steps ahead — identifying what’s needed to move faster and smarter.
   
   

Why So Shall We?

 - Great salary.

 - Work with great humans in a supportive, growth-oriented team that delivers real results.

 - Your personal development will be done directly with the CEO

 - Amazing office space in Palermo Hollywood

 - Opportunities for career development and professional growth.

 - Unlimited PTO.

source_jobs.title: UX/UI Designer (CRO)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SO SHALL WE:

We partner with mid‑market owners and executives in the US to drive bottom‑line growth with mutual upside. A rare combination of consulting, full-stack execution and aligned incentives to eliminate what is wrong with the agency model.

We are what remains. We make growth feel easy.

Because your growth is our business.




YOUR MISSION

Your mission as our UX/UI Designer (CRO Focus) is to raise conversion rates across our clients’ funnels through world-class, performance-driven design.

You’ll do this by combining sharp UX judgment, persuasive visual design, and a CRO mindset: auditing experiences, identifying friction, translating insights into clear hypotheses, and designing testable variants and scalable templates that help us ship faster — and learn faster.

You’ll partner closely with the Performance and Funnels teams to turn data and user behavior into shipped improvements and build a reusable design system that compounds results across accounts.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Landing Page Design & Optimization

 - Audit existing landing pages to identify UX friction, conversion blockers, and design opportunities.

 - Design high-converting, mobile-first landing pages and test variants in Figma.

 - Translate CRO hypotheses and experiment briefs into clear UI layouts and variants.

 - Improve visual hierarchy, messaging clarity, and user flow to increase conversion rates.

Experimentation & CRO Collaboration

 - Work closely with Performance teams to support A/B testing initiatives.

 - Design variants for experiments aimed at improving conversion rate and revenue.

 - Help translate insights from user behavior (funnels, heatmaps, session recordings) into design improvements.

 - Contribute to building scalable testing frameworks and reusable landing page patterns.

Execution & Collaboration

 - Partner with frontend developers to ensure designs are implemented accurately.

 - Maintain and evolve internal design systems and landing page templates to increase production speed.

 - Collaborate with performance team to ensure pages align with campaign goals and user intent.
   
   

Who You Are:

 - You have 4+ years of experience in UX/UI design or web design

 - You’ve designed landing pages for lead generation or eCommerce

 - You’re highly proficient in Figma (components, design systems, prototyping, grids)

 - You understand how design influences conversion, user behavior, and funnel performance

 - You enjoy working in fast-paced environments where ideas quickly move from concept to shipped pages



Why So Shall We?

 - Great salary.

 - Work with great humans in a supportive, growth-oriented team that delivers real results.

 - Your personal development will be done directly with the CEO

 - Amazing office space in Palermo Hollywood

 - Opportunities for career development and professional growth.

 - Unlimited PTO.

source_jobs.title: Growth Director
source_jobs.description:
About So Shall We

We partner with mid‑market owners and executives in the US to drive bottom‑line growth with mutual upside. A rare combination of consulting, full-stack execution and aligned incentives to eliminate what is wrong with the agency model.

We are what remains.

We make growth feel easy.

Because your growth is our business.

Your Mission

As a Growth Director, you’ll be the strategic architect and leader of a POD, overseeing the holistic growth engine for our key clients. You will be given complete ownership of the client’s growth roadmap. Your mission is to move beyond just paid media, leading a 360° strategy that integrates performance, CRO, retention, and business intelligence. You’ll be accountable for scaling businesses from a high-level perspective, managing and growing a multidisciplinary team to deliver sustainable, long-term impact. You will have ownership of your own P&L, with an incentive structure aligned to the performance of your business unit.



What You’ll Do

Growth Strategy & POD Leadership

 - Design and steer the 360° growth roadmap for clients, aligning performance marketing with overall business goals.

 - Lead and mentor a team of specialists (Performance, Creative, CRO), ensuring excellence in execution and a unified strategic direction.

 - Own high-level media planning and cross-channel budget allocation, optimizing for bottom-line profitability rather than just platform metrics.

Full-Funnel & Ecosystem Optimization

 - Oversee the entire customer journey, identifying growth levers in acquisition, activation, and retention to build a cohesive growth loop.

 - Drive the strategy for landing page architecture, funnel optimization, and CRM integration to maximize the efficiency of every dollar spent.

 - Collaborate with the CEO and senior stakeholders to refine the agency’s growth frameworks and proprietary methodologies.

Data Intelligence & Strategic Reporting

 - Translate complex data sets into high-level business insights, focusing on North Star metrics (MER, CAC Payback, LTV).

 - Supervise the creation of advanced tracking infrastructures and attribution models to ensure 100% transparency in performance.

 - Anticipate market trends and inefficiencies, pivoting the POD’s strategy to capitalize on new opportunities before the competition.

Client Partnership & Executive Management

 - Serve as the primary strategic consultant and "Growth Partner" for client Founders and C-level executives.

 - Lead high-stakes reporting sessions, building deep trust through strategic transparency and a relentless focus on business outcomes.

 - Act as the ultimate bridge between the client’s vision and the POD’s technical execution.

Innovation & Experimentation Culture

 - Champion a rigorous "test-and-learn" framework across the POD, prioritizing high-impact experiments that can be scaled across the portfolio.

 - Foster a culture of continuous innovation, staying at the forefront of AI, automation, and emerging growth tactics.



Who You Are

Business-Oriented Strategist: You think like a Founder. You understand that growth happens at the intersection of product, data, and marketing.

Senior Leader & Mentor: You have a proven track record of managing high-performing teams and helping specialists level up their strategic thinking.

Master Communicator: You excel at managing high-level stakeholders, turning complex technical challenges into clear, confident business narratives.

Technical Veteran: You come from a heavy Performance background. You’ve "been in the trenches" and can spot a tracking error or a weak creative hook from a mile away, but you now choose to lead the vision.

Commercially Minded: You understand the realities of a P&L, the difference between cost and value, and how to think about the growth of revenue for both your clients, and your pod.



Why So Shall We?

 - Great salary.

 - Work with great humans in a supportive, growth-oriented team that delivers real results.

 - Your

source_jobs.title: Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SO SHALL WE:

We partner with mid‑market owners and executives in the US to drive bottom‑line growth with mutual upside. A rare combination of consulting, full-stack execution and aligned incentives to eliminate what is wrong with the agency model.

We are what remains. We make growth feel easy.

Because your growth is our business.




YOUR MISSION:

As an Account Manager, you’ll own client onboarding, delivery cadence, and cross-functional coordination to drive on-time, on-scope work, client satisfaction/retention, and measurable business outcomes. You’ll be trusted with end-to-end ownership of assigned accounts and supported by Performance, Creative, CRO, and Data to deliver clear visibility, proactive guidance, and smooth launches. This role blends structured planning with hands-on execution and requires excellent communication, stakeholder management, and delivery discipline to scale impact across multiple clients and pods.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Client Leadership & Relationship Management

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for assigned clients, build strong relationships, and provide exceptional service.
   

 - Conduct regular client meetings and check-ins to review performance, discuss strategy, and address questions or concerns.
   

 - Manage client expectations and foster long-term relationships.

Client Onboarding & Activation

 - Lead the onboarding process: gather requirements, set expectations, and coordinate with internal teams to ensure smooth campaign launches.

Strategic Guidance & Recommendations

 - Develop and maintain a deep understanding of client goals, objectives, and challenges, providing strategic guidance to drive campaign success.

Cross-Functional Coordination

 - Collaborate with performance marketers, designers, copywriters, and analysts to execute campaigns aligned with client business objectives.

Performance Reporting & Optimization

 - Provide regular updates and transparent reporting on campaign performance, including key metrics, insights, and actionable recommendations for optimization.

Industry Trends & Best Practices

 - Stay up-to-date with industry trends, platform updates, and performance marketing best practices, sharing insights and recommendations to drive continuous improvement.

Delivery Management (Time & Budget)

 - Ensure deliverables are met on time and within budget.




WHO YOU ARE:

Experienced Account Leader

 - 5+ years in account management or client services (ideally in a digital marketing agency or related industry).

Clear Communicator

 - Able to articulate complex ideas to both technical and non-technical audiences.

Highly Organized, Detail-Oriented Operator

 - Manages multiple client accounts simultaneously while meeting deadlines and maintaining high quality standards.

Relationship-Builder & Retention Driver

 - Proven record of exceptional client service, strong relationships, and driving satisfaction and retention.

Bachelor’s-Degreed Professional

 - Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field.

Performance Marketing Practitioner

 - Strong background in performance marketing; solid understanding of digital advertising (Meta Ads, Google Ads is a plus).




WHY SO SHALL WE?

 - Great salary.

 - Work with great humans in a supportive, growth-oriented team that delivers real results.

 - Your personal development will be done directly with the CEO

 - Amazing office space in Palermo Hollywood

 - Opportunities for career development and professional growth.

 - Unlimited PTO.

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SO SHALL WE:

We partner with mid‑market owners and executives in the US to drive bottom‑line growth with mutual upside. A rare combination of consulting, full-stack execution and aligned incentives to eliminate what is wrong with the agency model.

We are what remains. We make growth feel easy.

Because your growth is our business.



YOUR MISSION

As our Executive Assistant, you’ll provide high-level operational, administrative, and hospitality support across the business — helping executives stay organized, ensuring key internal processes run smoothly, and creating thoughtful employee experiences along the way. You’ll act as a reliable go-to for coordination, follow-through, and problem solving, bringing structure, discretion, and a strong service mindset to both executive support and day-to-day workplace needs.

 
 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

Executive Support

 - Manage and fulfill day-to-day requests from executives, ensuring priorities are handled efficiently and with attention to detail.

 - Handle executive calendars and email inboxes, helping keep schedules, communications, and follow-ups organized.

 - Track action items from executive meetings and ensure they are followed through to completion.

 - Coordinate reservations, travel arrangements, and bookings for executives and remote team members when needed.

 - Maintain strict confidentiality when handling sensitive information.

 - Support internal processes such as survey completion (360s, pulse surveys, and similar initiatives).

 - Track and organize partner and executive expenses.

Event & Experience Coordination

 - Support the organization of offsites, retreats, internal meetings, and company events.

 - Coordinate logistics and details to help create smooth, thoughtful team experiences.

Employee Support

 - Own and keep the employee leave calendar up to date.

 - Schedule personal development sessions for the CEO.

 - Maintain employee and equipment trackers accurately and consistently.

 - Support internal HR and people initiatives such as Let’s Connect! and similar programs.



WHO YOU ARE

Organized & Detail-Oriented

You’re highly organized, dependable, and able to manage multiple moving pieces without losing track of details.

Hospitality-Driven

You naturally think in terms of service and experience, and you care about making things feel smooth, thoughtful, and well handled for others.

Proactive Problem-Solver

You’re resourceful, practical, and quick to step in when something needs attention or coordination.

Discreet & Trustworthy

You handle sensitive information with professionalism, maturity, and sound judgment.

Strong Communicator

You communicate clearly and confidently in both English and Spanish, and you’re comfortable working with executives, employees, and external stakeholders.




REQUIREMENTS

 - 2+ years of experience in concierge services, hospitality, executive support, operations, or similar coordination-heavy roles.

 - Excellent organizational and multitasking abilities.

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills in both English and Spanish.

 - High attention to detail and a proactive, problem-solving mindset.

 - Strong service orientation and ability to handle a wide range of requests with professionalism.



WHY SO SHALL WE?

 - Great salary.

 - Work with great humans in a supportive, growth-oriented team.

 - Amazing office space in Palermo Hollywood.

 - Opportunities for growth and development.

 - Unlimited PTO.

source_jobs.title: SEO Specialist
source_jobs.description:
AEO & SEO Specialist | SO SHALL WE | JOB DESCRIPTION




ABOUT SO SHALL WE:

We partner with mid‑market owners and executives in the US to drive bottom‑line growth with mutual upside. A rare combination of consulting, full-stack execution and aligned incentives to eliminate what is wrong with the agency model.

We are what remains. We make growth feel easy.

Because your growth is our business.




YOUR MISSION

Own and elevate combined AEO & SEO performance across a portfolio of accounts, with clear priorities and measurable outcomes — while proactively expanding our capability into AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) as search behavior evolves. This role is for someone senior enough to set the standard for how we execute SEO, and curious enough to explore what “organic growth” looks like in a world of AI answers. This is for someone who is ready to build a team underneath them and has desires for personal growth.



What you’ll do

 - Own AEO & SEO strategy and performance for a portfolio of accounts, with clear priorities and measurable outcomes.
   

 - Lead audits and diagnose what’s actually holding growth back (technical, content, structure), then drive the plan to fix it.
   

 - Set direction for content and page strategy (what to build, what to refresh, what to consolidate, what to kill), aligned to intent and conversion.
   

 - Partner closely with Creative, Strategy & Position, and CRO to get initiatives shipped end-to-end (briefs, requirements, QA, measurement).
   

 - Build an operating cadence: roadmap, weekly priorities, clear updates, and a repeatable way of working.
   

 - Translate performance into simple insights and next steps for internal teams and clients.
   

 - Raise the bar: improve standards, templates, documentation, and processes so SEO output compounds over time.
   

 - Proactively research and test AEO opportunities across accounts (where answers surface, what influences visibility, and what to do about it).
   

 - Track and share learnings: define what success looks like, what to measure, and how we report progress as the landscape changes.
   

 - Turn experiments into a repeatable approach we can apply across the portfolio.



What we’re looking for

 - Strong technical + strategic SEO foundation, and the judgment to prioritize what matters.
   

 - Senior execution ability: you can run workstreams end-to-end and influence cross-functional teams without needing heavy management.
   

 - Commercial mindset: you connect SEO work to demand, pipeline, or revenue — not just rankings.
   

 - Deep curiosity for how organic discovery is evolving, having already taken steps to implement AEO in your current role.
   

 - Comfortable with ambiguity and high ownership; you create clarity and momentum.
   

Nice to have

 - Experience in the US market, lead gen, or ecommerce.
   

 - SEO + CRO fluency (landing pages, experimentation, conversion-minded content).
   

 - Agency or portfolio experience owning SEO across multiple accounts and stakeholders.
   

 - Hands-on experience with AEO (e.g., optimizing for featured snippets/PAAs, entity/intent clarity, structured data, or testing visibility across AI answer surfaces) — even if it’s early-stage experiments.



Why So Shall We?

 - Great salary.

 - Work with great humans in a supportive, growth-oriented team that delivers real results.

 - Your personal development will be done directly with the CEO

 - Amazing office space in Palermo Hollywood

 - Opportunities for career development and professional growth.

 - Unlimited PTO.

source_jobs.title: Lead Video Editor & Motion Graphics
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SO SHALL WE:

We partner with mid‑market owners and executives in the US to drive bottom‑line growth with mutual upside. A rare combination of consulting, full-stack execution, and aligned incentives to eliminate what is wrong with the agency model.

We are what remains. We make growth feel easy.

Because your growth is our business.



YOUR MISSION

As our Lead Video Editor, you’ll own the creation and evolution of high-performing video content across organic and paid channels.

You’ll be responsible for transforming raw footage into clear, engaging, and conversion-driven videos—balancing storytelling, pacing, and performance insights to drive real results.

You’ll work closely with Growth, Creative, and Brand teams to define and scale So Shall We’s visual identity, while continuously improving output quality, speed, and consistency through systems and AI-driven workflows.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Edit and deliver short-form video content (2–3 per day) across platforms like TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts.

 - Produce long-form content (1–2 per week) for YouTube, podcasts, or other brand and client assets.

 - Translate briefs, creative direction, and performance inputs into clear, engaging video edits.

 - Apply editing techniques that improve retention, pacing, clarity, and storytelling.

 - Collaborate closely with Growth and Creative teams to align content with campaign objectives and hooks.

 - Select and integrate music, b-roll, and visual elements that enhance narrative and brand identity.

 - Use AI tools and automation to improve editing efficiency, output, and consistency.

 - Analyze performance data and feedback to iterate on structure, pacing, and creative direction.

 - Contribute to and help define internal editing systems, templates, and best practices.

 - Provide guidance and feedback to junior editors as the team grows.

 - Stay up to date with trends, platform dynamics, and content formats to ensure relevance.




WHO YOU ARE

 - 3+ years of experience editing video content for social media, digital marketing, or content platforms.

 - Strong experience in both short-form and long-form editing.

 - Advanced proficiency in tools like Premiere Pro, Final Cut, DaVinci Resolve, After Effects, or similar.

 - Strong understanding of pacing, storytelling, and retention mechanics in high-performing content.

 - Experience working with performance-driven content (organic or paid).

 - Familiarity with AI-based editing tools or strong willingness to adopt them.

 - Strong sense of visual taste, trends, and platform-native content styles.

 - Comfortable working in a fast-paced, feedback-driven environment.

 - Able to explain creative decisions clearly and collaborate cross-functionally.

 - Good English communication skills (written and spoken).




WHY SO SHALL WE?

 - Great salary (base salary + performance-based incentives).

 - Work directly with leadership on high-impact content and brand initiatives.

 - Be part of a team that values quality, speed, and continuous improvement.

 - Opportunity to shape and scale the internal media function.

 - Clear path toward leadership as the team grows.

 - Unlimited PTO.

source_jobs.title: Junior Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SO SHALL WE:

We partner with mid‑market owners and executives in the US to drive bottom‑line growth with mutual upside. A rare combination of consulting, full-stack execution and aligned incentives to eliminate what is wrong with the agency model.

We are what remains. We make growth feel easy.

Because your growth is our business.




YOUR MISSION:

As a Junior Account Manager, you’ll manage day-to-day client communication and ensure alignment across Performance, Creative, and clients.

You’ll play a key role in keeping projects organized, translating client needs into clear direction, and acting as the bridge between clients, Creative, and Performance from brief to execution.

Working closely with senior team members, you’ll help drive strategy into clear, high-quality deliverables delivered on time.

This is a hands-on role at the intersection of client services, project management, and performance marketing — ideal for someone proactive, detail-oriented, and eager to grow in a fast-paced environment.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Client Communication & Relationship Management

 - Manage daily communication with clients, ensuring clarity, responsiveness, and strong alignment.
   Build trust through organized follow-ups, clear updates, and a strong understanding of client goals.

Project & Delivery Coordination

 - Act as the link between clients and internal teams (Creative, Performance, Strategy).
   Ensure alignment across goals, priorities, timelines, and deliverables.
   Track tasks and timelines, ensuring work is delivered on time and flagging risks early.

Client Onboarding & Briefing

 - Support client onboarding processes, ensuring all key information is gathered and organized.
   Build clear, structured briefs that guide internal teams toward cohesive and effective execution.

Strategy Execution Alignment

 - Ensure that strategic direction is clearly translated into execution across all teams.
   Help maintain consistency between strategy, messaging, and final outputs.

Content & Social Direction Support

 - Collaborate on content direction for platforms like Instagram and TikTok.
   Stay up-to-date with market and cultural trends to inform creative angles and messaging.



Who You Are:

Strong Communicator

 - Clear and structured communication, both written and verbal, with the ability to coordinate across multiple stakeholders.

Highly Organized & Detail-Oriented

 - Able to manage multiple tasks and timelines while maintaining clarity and quality.

Proactive & Ownership-Oriented

 - Takes initiative, anticipates needs, and follows through on tasks without constant supervision.

Team Player

 - Comfortable working cross-functionally and collaborating with different teams.

Interest in Performance Marketing

 - Familiarity or strong interest in paid media (Meta Ads) and digital marketing fundamentals.

Curious & Growth-Oriented

 - Eager to learn, improve, and develop within a high-performance environment.

Previous experience in similar roles or exposure to marketing/digital environments is a plus.



Why So Shall We?

 - Great salary.

 - Work with great humans in a supportive, growth-oriented team that delivers real results.

 - Your personal development will be done directly with the CEO

 - Amazing office space in Palermo Hollywood

 - Opportunities for career development and professional growth.

 - Unlimited PTO.

source_jobs.title: Funnel & Web Developer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SO SHALL WE:

We partner with mid‑market owners and executives in the US to drive bottom‑line growth with mutual upside. A rare combination of consulting, full-stack execution, and aligned incentives to eliminate what is wrong with the agency model.

We are what remains. We make growth feel easy.

Because your growth is our business.



YOUR MISSION

You’ll own the execution of high-performance landing pages and funnel experiences that turn strategy into something real, fast, and conversion-focused.

This role sits at the intersection of marketing, web execution, and systems thinking. You won’t be expected to write production-grade software, but you should think like someone who understands how things connect, break, scale, and convert.

Your mission is to move quickly without hand-holding, build clean and effective web experiences, and flex into integrations, automations, and CRM-related work whenever the funnel requires it.



WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Build, edit, and QA landing pages across platforms (GoHighLevel, Unbounce, AI-assisted builders like Claude Code / Replit)

 - Implement tracking, form logic, UTM routing, redirects, and pixel setup without needing a brief for every step

 - Own funnel infrastructure: page flow, thank you pages, upsell sequences, confirmation logic

 - Handle light-to-medium integration work — webhooks, Zapier/n8n, API connections — and know when to escalate vs. figure it out

 - Translate client or AM briefs into working pages with minimal back-and-forth

 - Use AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, etc.) as a force multiplier — you know how to prompt well and verify output



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Must-have

 - Solid track record building landing pages that actually shipped — GHL, Unbounce, Webflow, or similar

 - Strong HTML/CSS hands, comfortable touching JS when needed

 - You can read a webhook payload, trace a broken Zap, or debug a form submission without panicking

 - Understands how APIs work conceptually — even if you're not writing them, you know what's being passed and why

 - Self-directed: you scope your own work, flag blockers early, and don't wait to be told what to do next

 - AI-native workflow — you use it daily, not occasionally

Nice to have

 - GoHighLevel experience specifically (page builder + automation side)

 - Exposure to Meta/Google Ads funnel infrastructure (CAPI, UTMs, pixel events)

 - Any backend exposure: n8n, Zapier, basic API calls

 - Performance marketing agency background



WHAT THIS IS NOT

A senior engineering role. You won't be architecting systems or managing infrastructure. But you will be expected to operate independently, own your output, and bring solutions — not just problems.



WHY SO SHALL WE?

 - Great salary.

 - Work with great humans in a supportive, growth-oriented team that delivers real results.

 - Your personal development will be done directly with the CEO

 - Amazing office space in Palermo.

 - Opportunities for career development and professional growth.

 - Unlimited PTO.

source_jobs.title: Creative Strategist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SO SHALL WE:

We partner with mid‑market owners and executives in the US to drive bottom‑line growth with mutual upside. A rare combination of consulting, full-stack execution, and aligned incentives to eliminate what is wrong with the agency model.

We are what remains. We make growth feel easy.

Because your growth is our business.



YOUR MISSION

As a Creative Strategist, you’ll own concepts and copy across ads, landing pages, and funnels (email/SMS) to drive meaningful, profitable growth for mid-market US brands.

You’ll be a creative partner inside our creative team: you focus on ideas and words; your full-stack creative partner focuses on execution and production (static and video). Together, you’ll turn insights and performance learnings into clear, compelling creative that helps people understand, trust, and buy.

You’ll sit in the creative team and report to our founder, working at the intersection of creative and strategic positioning as the company continues to scale.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own concepts and copy for:

 - Ads (hooks, angles, body copy, CTAs, scripts for video, statics and carousels).

 - Landing pages and thank-you pages (hero, sections, proof, objections, CTAs).

 - Email and SMS flows and campaigns where needed.

 - Turn customer problems, objections and desires into clear angles, hooks and messages using our performance-oriented creative frameworks.

 - Think and write in proven performance structures (benefit-first, problem → solution, UGC/testimonials, demos, before-and-after, comparisons, story-style, etc.) and adapt them to different brands.

 - Use AI as leverage to generate and refine a high volume of variations (hooks, angles, scripts, CTAs) while keeping ownership of the strategy and final output.

 - Look at creative performance with intent (what happens after the click, not just CTR) and suggest concrete next tests: new angles, proof, offers, formats or structures.

 - Collaborate day-to-day with your full-stack creative partner and performance marketers so ads, landing pages and funnels tell a consistent story end-to-end.

 - Help maintain and improve our internal templates, frameworks and playbooks so the creative team can move faster and learn from what works.
   


WHO YOU ARE:

 - Fluent, high-level English copywriter. You can think and write in English at a near-native level; this is non-negotiable as all creative and client work is in English.

 - Performance-minded creative. You care about strong ideas, but you care even more about whether they help improve results.

 - Strategic and structured. You naturally think in flows and frameworks (problem, solution, proof, offer) and can connect creative decisions to business outcomes.

 - AI-first and experimentation-driven. You’re comfortable using AI daily and testing many variations, using data as input for your next iteration.

 - Collaborative partner. You enjoy working closely with creatives and performance marketers, treating the relationship as a true creative partnership.

 - Organised and reliable. You can handle multiple brands and deadlines without dropping quality or communication.
   


NICE TO HAVE:

 - Experience in performance marketing, content strategy or direct-response copywriting (agency, freelance or in-house).

 - Hands-on work with Meta as a performance channel, and exposure to funnels and landing pages.
   


WHY SO SHALL WE?

 - Great salary.

 - Work with great humans in a supportive, growth-oriented team that delivers real results.

 - Your personal development will be done directly with the CEO

 - Amazing office space in Palermo Hollywood

 - Opportunities for career development and professional growth.

 - Unlimited PTO.

notes: Mostly marketing and sales roles, with a few creative and technical positions.
